response.justification: Candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and some embedded-adjacent work, but the role demands native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX systems programming, Linux kernel modification experience, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP). The resume shows no Japanese language ability, no kernel-level work, minimal recent C programming (last meaningful C/C++ stint was at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and no embedded networking or switch security background. The hard requirements gap, particularly Japanese fluency, is disqualifying.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) as a senior/staff engineer across diverse domains
Some historical C/C++ experience at National Instruments and exposure to Linux/Jack audio systems
Strong testing, TDD, and software craftsmanship background useful for quality-critical embedded work
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed (hard requirement: native-level)
No Linux kernel source modification, embedded firmware, or switch/networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP)
Recent career is web/cloud/TypeScript-focused; C/UNIX systems work is over a decade stale
